en.4723b2ab: A food web shows how energy is ditributed. Do you remember the order it went?
en.18bb35e0: Oops! That's not it. Remember: Rabbits are herbivores and only eat plants.
en.456a45f8: Use WS or the arrow keys to control the ship, you can also use the joystick iff you are on mobile!
en.af33d1fd: Flying or collinding will make you lose fuel and if it reaches 0 you will pass out. Collect fuel to continue exploring!
en.61f2ea4f: Be careful of obstacles! Failling to avoid them will damage your ship and make you lose fuel!
en.d781322b: Barriers can have extra food or a predator! Avoid predators because they can hurt you.
en.6d3a76a9: Now you are playing as a predator, this means other animals are their prey and serve as food to them. Did you know that?
en.a6c1d448: The fox is a predator but it's also a OMNIVORE. This means it eats both meat and plants! The bunny can only eat plants, it means it is a HERBIVORE. Amazing right?
en.d0836203: The wolf is a carnivore and a predator! This means it can only eat meat from other animals.
en.07e132a4: You finished your first ecosystem! That's amazing! Let's review what we learned?
en.8eddf514: That's right! Energy starts in the grass and goes all the way to the wolf! Those are all living creatures that need energy to survive meaning they depend on each other!
en.5024dc20: Oops! That's not it. Remember: Foxes are omnivores, they can eat both meat and plants.
en.55fc1af1: Oops! That's not it. Remember: Wolves are carnivores, they can eat only meat.
en.40f4585a: Did you know that all energy starts with the sun? Let's understand it!
en.b055f3d8: The food web happens in every ecosystem in the planet! Let's understand how it starts?
en.5fd301ff: Oops! That's not it. Bushes are producers and they get their energy from the sun!
en.45d7a0c0: Oops! That's not it. Hawks are apex predators meaning they come last in the food web!
en.f460ac72: Oops! That's not it. Road Runners are predators and get their energy from preys!
en.9f0b0de8: Oops! That's not it. Beetles are prey and they only get their energy from plants.
en.e8000872: Plants are called Producers because they produce energy from the light they receive from the sun, without them all living beings would not have energy to live!
en.5790cb3a: Now we know what producers are, but what about consumers? Can you complete the food web so we can discover?
en.905931d1: You got it! Consumers are living beings that can't produce energy for themselfs and rely on others to get the energy they need.
en.65a58a88: Now let's complete the food web and learn more about Preoducers and Consumers!
en.9e0622d2: Inside the sea there are a lot of different producers, like...
en.8f3793dc: Plants are called Producers because they produce energy from the light they receive from the sun, without them all living beings would not have energy to live!
en.2c1b2210: Phytoplanktons are abundat in the sea, they are small organisms that float freally and serve as food to Zooplanktons. They're the base of the ocean web.
en.f9036a7c: Zooplanktons may look like producers but they feed off Phytoplanktons, being considered Primary Consumers.
en.650cdf2d-0c3f-44b6-9f4d-b6fb844fb5a7: Now that you understand the beginning of the ocean food web, complete the rest.
en.afe39695: Now that you understand the base of the ocean's food web, complete the rest of it to learn more:
en.e742de64-15f9-4180-8f8e-3a512f3f5e2d: Now let's complete the food web and learn more about Producers and Consumers!
en.5f890d18: Now let's complete the food web and learn more about Producers and Consumers!
en.57bc644d: You got it! There's an infinite amount of food web variations in every ecosystem, the ones we showed you are just a few examples.
en.d6cfa176: Oops! That's not it. Small fish feed mainly off Zooplanktons due to their size, they are secondary consumers.
en.934a961d: Oops! That's not it. Sharks are carnivore animals and tertiary consumers.
en.5f11cd46: Oops! That's not it. An Orca Whale is an apex predator, being the last on the food web, they are the last consumers here.
en.7a0fc7e1: Did you know that Apex predators are not the end of a food web? We also have the Decomposers, composed of Fungi and Bacteria!
en.5e46878d: But what is a Decomposer? When a plant or animal dies, Decomposers consume their nutrients and give them back to the soil, starting the foodweb all over again!
en.03a37d55: Now that you know so much, let's understand how the food web ends!
en.5d60a28e: Oops! That's not it! Bears are apex predators, they should be before decomposers.
en.0dbb9a9f: Oops! That's not it. Decomposers break down dead plants and animals to give back to earth.
en.02ad9d82: You got it! Decomposers act in various levels of the food webs, everything that is dead will go through a decomposer and turn into nutrients for the soil.
en.fbed9b88: Use the space bar or button  do tricks to gain a few extra points.
en.847db0db: But first let's get to Earth! Be careful there's a lot of obstacles on the way there. The space is dangerous!
en.1f382790: Finally Earth! Everything is beauriful here, let's be careful to not colide with anything okay?
en.3096c17a: Wow the biosphere is soo diverse! Let's learn more about it as we explore!
en.48c14238: How the hydrophere sure is huge in Earth! Look at the size of this sea!
en.055bfb52: Below the sea is like a whole other world, so many new ecosystems and organisms live here. I'm so excited to explore!
en.599d7e46: We are inside the geosphere right now, let's be suuuper careful here!
en.129d0826: All right, we just saw water flowing down over the mountain, that is the effect of runoff!
en.55291c39: In this simulation, you will be able to see how runoff happens!
en.ea904717: Now click on the “pin” to remove it and let the water flow.
en.b6845667: In this simulation, you will be able to see how runoff happens!
en.3ad4bfa2: Good job! See how the excess water ran through the tube and then stayed on the flask on the right?
en.706fa1d1: That is just like what you saw in the last level, where the rain affected the water creating the runoff effect over the surface.
en.dd3eb617: This time we just saw water evaporate!
en.2a35e54b: In this simulation, you will be able to see how evaporation happens.
en.a34a7569: All right, now click on the flame icon to heat up the water!
en.65ab7687: Good job! See how the water turned into vapor? That’s the evaporation process happening!
en.aa95d194: All right, let’s go back on swiming, we have a lot to learn!
en.d01d7ca2: Ok, so now we just saw how the evaporated water can condense in a cloud to become rain later!
en.cb460cf5: You already know how to make water evaporate. Now try taking the vapor to that ice cube, so we can see condensation happening.
en.9d0eb873: In this simulation, you will be able to see how the rain happens.
en.483796e8: Take the vapor to that ice cube
en.c841a884: That’s so awesome! You simulated water running off the tube, then it evaporated and then it condensed back to water again, just like rain!
en.cf6840d0: I’m learning so much thanks to you! Let’s go!
en.80aabc4a: All right, we just saw water freeze and turn into snow!
en.6b740144: That flask on the left is emitting really cold particles, let’s see what it does to the liquid water.
en.60e5ec8c: That’s right! When water’s temperature is too low, it freezes and turns into ice.
en.021c2164: That is why when precipitation happens sometimes it may be snow too!
en.93e70fe9: That is why when precipitation happens sometimes it may be snow too!
